metadata.description: Expanded real earthquake ground truth: 25 original + 36 new from USGS/INGV catalogs

events.name: 2016 Kaikōura, New Zealand Earthquake
events.location: North Canterbury, 53 km NNE of Amberley

events.name: 2019 Ridgecrest, USA Earthquake
events.location: Searles Valley, Naval Air Weapons Station China Lake, California

events.name: 2023 Türkiye-Syria Earthquakes (M7.8 + M7.5)
events.notes: Largest event in dataset by casualties and building damage. M7.5 aftershock occurred 9 hours later. 402 km rupture length.

events.name: 2021 Fukushima, Japan Earthquake
events.location: 73 km ENE of Namie, offshore east coast Honshu
events.notes: Deep (44km) intermediate-depth earthquake; limited damage despite M7.1 magnitude due to depth and modern building codes.

events.name: 2011 Christchurch, New Zealand Earthquake
events.location: Port Hills region, southeast of Christchurch CBD
events.notes: 3rd most expensive earthquake in recorded history. Peak ground acceleration 2.2g (Heathcote Valley). 67% of CBD buildings subsequently demolished.

events.name: 2004 Sumatra-Andaman, Indonesia (Indian Ocean) Earthquake
events.location: Off west coast of Aceh, northern Sumatra, Indonesia
events.notes: 2nd or 3rd most powerful earthquake since modern seismography (1900). Longest fault rupture ever observed (1200-1300 km). Deadliest tsunami in recorded history. Rupture duration ~10 minutes (longest recorded).

events.name: 2015 Nepal Earthquake
events.location: 82 km NW of Kathmandu, Gorkha District
events.notes: 33% of Nepal's 2014/15 GDP. 20,000+ landslides triggered across 25,000+ km². Rupture length ~75 miles.

events.name: 2012 Emilia, Italy Earthquakes (M5.9 + M5.8)
events.notes: Significant industrial damage; 300,000 cheese wheels destroyed (~EUR 200M in Grana Padano/Parmigiano-Reggiano losses).

events.name: 2019 Albania Earthquake
events.notes: Peak ground acceleration 192 cm/s² (1.92g) in Durrës. Liquefaction and site amplification major factors.

events.name: 2023 Morocco Earthquake
events.location: Ighil, Al Haouz Province, 73 km SW of Marrakech

events.name: 1999 Athens, Greece Earthquake
events.location: Mount Parnitha, 20 km NW of Athens
events.notes: First severe earthquake in Athens in 2,500+ years.

events.name: 2016 Kumamoto, Japan Earthquakes (M6.2, M6.0, M7.0)
events.notes: Deadliest in Japan since 2011 Tōhoku. Rupture length ~40 km; surface offset >2m. Foreshock sequence (M6.2, M6.0) preceded M7.0 mainshock.

events.name: 2017 Chiapas, Mexico Earthquake
events.location: Gulf of Tehuantepec, 87 km SW of Pijijiapan

events.name: 2017 Puebla, Mexico Earthquake
events.notes: 228 deaths in Mexico City; 38 mid-rise structures collapsed.

events.name: 2018 Lombok, Indonesia Earthquake
events.location: North Lombok, 3 km SE Loloan Village
events.notes: Deadliest in Lesser Sunda Islands since 1992 Flores earthquake. >90% of buildings damaged/destroyed in North Lombok.

events.name: 2018 Palu, Indonesia Earthquake
events.location: 70 km N of Palu City, Sulawesi
events.notes: Tsunami 4-7m height. Largest liquefaction event in world (liquefaction depth ~16m). Left-lateral strike-slip.

events.name: 2020 Petrinja, Croatia Earthquake
events.notes: Sisak-Moslavina County 79% of losses. Cathedral damage significant. Unreinforced masonry most vulnerable.

events.name: 2022 Fukushima, Japan Earthquake (Namie M7.3)
events.location: 57 km ENE of Namie, offshore east coast Honshu
events.notes: Intra-plate earthquake in subducting Pacific plate (57km depth). Foreshock M6.4 occurred 2 min before mainshock.

events.name: 2008 Wenchuan, China Earthquake
events.mag_type: Mw (reported as M8.0 by Chinese agencies)
events.location: 50 miles W-NW of Chengdu, Sichuan Province

events.name: 2012 Indian Ocean Earthquake (M8.6)
events.location: 610 km SW of Banda Aceh, Indonesia
events.notes: Largest intraplate strike-slip earthquake ever recorded. Fault displacement 21.3m (70 ft). M8.2 aftershock 2h 5m later. Remote triggering of M≥5.5 events increased 5-fold for 6 days post-event.
